title: brassica oleracea subsp. gemmifera
intro: Brassica oleracea subsp. gemmifera, also known as Brussels sprouts, is a cool-season crop belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its compact, immature buds that resemble small cabbages. The medicinal properties of Brussels sprouts include their high content of vitamins C and K, as well as their ability to stimulate the production of glutathione, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of consuming Brussels sprouts include reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, as well as supporting digestive health and immune function. Brussels sprouts can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, soups, and sautés, and can also be taken as a supplement in capsule or powder form.
